description: Manifest for the remaining-tmux-dependency audit (roadmap INT-008 / Seq 1251). Hand-maintained: boundary + taxonomy + classification rules + per-file overrides. The full inventory (inventory.json) and summary (inventory.md) are generated from this file by generate.ts; the deterministic check re-derives from it.

boundary.note: git-tracked files only. Detection is the literal token `tmux` (case-insensitive), a complete file-level signal here (verified: no tmux grammar exists without it outside the adapter tests, which the grammar signal still catches).
boundary.historicalNote: Append-only ship history (changelogs) and immutable ADRs are counted as known references but never inventoried or checked, because they are not edited to remove tmux.

categories.terminal-ownership: Owns tmux sessions/server lifecycle, config, themes, and adapter core.
categories.pane-layout: Panes, windows, splits, layout, zoom, focus, and the tmux prefix cheat sheet.
categories.capture-state: capture-pane, scrollback, copy-mode, search, and terminal state parsing.
categories.process-resource: Reads the tmux process/pane tree for port and resource attribution.
categories.agent-control: Agent-facing tmux grammar (send-keys to agent panes, prompts, skill content).
categories.cli-rpc-ui: CLI commands, RPC handlers, menus, and UI wiring that speak tmux.
categories.packaging-update: Bundled tmux binary, shim, staging, signing, CI, container, and app-bundle copy.
categories.compatibility: Backward-compatible data, session naming, and backend-identity contracts.
categories.test-only: Tests, e2e, and fixtures; removed with the production code they cover.
categories.documentation: Live docs, user-facing copy, and agent instructions naming tmux.

roadmap.LAY-003: Connect logical pane IDs to native host panes.
roadmap.LAY-007: Verify desktop, browser, and narrow/mobile rendering.
roadmap.MIG-006: Keep tmux failure isolation (native replacement asserts non-use).
roadmap.RUN-006: Package, sign, and update the host.
roadmap.CUT-006: Prove deletion mechanically (no user-facing tmux copy).

depths.deep-internal: Inside the tmux adapter (src/bun/tmux/**) — owns tmux vocabulary by design.
depths.caller: Production code outside the adapter that calls it or otherwise depends on tmux.
depths.surface: Docs, user-facing copy, CSS, packaging scripts, CI, and config that name tmux but are not TS callers.
depths.isolation: Native replacement / spike that references tmux only to assert it does NOT use it.

dependencyKinds.active: Removing tmux forces a behavior/output change here.
dependencyKinds.reference: Only a mention (comment, doc, copy, log key); deletable as cleanup.

rules.consumer: Localized user-facing copy naming tmux (sessions, cheat sheet, action buttons, tooltips).
rules.deletionPrerequisite: The named feature is migrated/removed in its own roadmap item; copy updated in the same change (CUT-005/CUT-006).

rules.consumer: Test / e2e / fixture exercising or asserting tmux behavior or isolation.
rules.deletionPrerequisite: Removed together with the production code it exercises.

rules.consumer: Backend-neutral parity corpus + the tmux runner proving the current backend satisfies it (test artifact only; no production imports).
rules.deletionPrerequisite: Corpus retargets the native runner (MIG-001); the tmux runner is removed with the tmux backend (CUT-005).

rules.consumer: Native single-view adapter: names tmux only in comments asserting it never touches a tmux session, and to contrast its harness with the corpus's tmux runner.
rules.deletionPrerequisite: None — the module references tmux only to assert it does NOT use tmux; it survives tmux removal unchanged.

src/bun/tmux/client.ts.consumer: TmuxClient + `tmux` singleton: typed subcommands (new/has/kill/attach-session, split-window, select-pane, send-keys, capture-pane, copy-mode, list-panes).
src/bun/tmux/client.ts.deletionPrerequisite: Native backend is the proven default on all platforms (CUT-003) and no supported session needs tmux (CUT-004/CUT-005).

src/bun/tmux/binary.ts.consumer: tmux binary selection, PATH-shim dereference/sanitize, version probe (bundled/vendored/PATH resolution).
src/bun/tmux/binary.ts.deletionPrerequisite: Native runtime ships and no supported build bundles or installs tmux (RUN-006/CUT-005).
